This does not choose Link Movement or judge quality, but it weakens fail-closed admission at the adapter surface.

missing_or_mismatched_facts: Mismatch against the stated closed-tier claim: agent_request_read_tier uses intersection with READ_ONLY_TOOL_POLICY_REFS, so a mixed unknown-plus-admitted policy request reaches read instead of none.
Checker gap: _agent_read_tier_probe covers reviewer-readonly positive, leader-coordination positive, read-write-only none, and Gemini none, but does not cover unknown-plus-admitted ambiguity.

observed_evidence: BROKEN: one adversarial edge does not hold. A direct AgentAdapterRequest with tool_policy_refs=("tool-policy:unknown", "tool-policy:reviewer-readonly") entered the read tier and rendered read-only inspection rules instead of failing closed to none.

Direct probe output for that edge: unknown-only -> read false and no-tools; unknown-plus-reviewer -> read true with read-only repository inspection rules; read-write-only -> read false; leader-plus-readwrite -> read true.

Full lane sweep over the 8 active Agent Objects observed expected nominal behavior: coo/pm-lead/design-lead/cto-lead/qa-lead/qa/inspector non-write requests enter read on codex-local and claude-local; gemini-local and chat-session stay none; dev non-write stays none. Write-scope requests for pm-lead/design-lead/cto-lead/dev/qa-lead on codex-local or claude-local enter write, not read.

Artifact-grounding trigger probe observed axis-attack-qa, code-attack-qa, evidence-integrity, and review trigger evidence_used grounding; design triggers evidence_refs grounding; closure triggers no grounding field.
